hooks.command: input=$(cat); file_path=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('tool_input',{}).get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$file_path" ] && echo "$file_path" | grep -q '\.py$'; then cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)" && (ruff check --fix --quiet -- "$file_path" 2>/dev/null; ruff format --quiet -- "$file_path" 2>/dev/null); fi; exit 0

input=$(cat); file_path=$(echo "$input" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('tool_input',{}).get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null); root=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd); cd "$root" || exit 0; if [ ! -f .pre-commit-config.yaml ] || [ -z "$file_path" ]; then exit 0; fi; case "$file_path" in *.md|*.yml|*.yaml) ;; *) exit 0;; esac; pre-commit run --files "$file_path" 2>/dev/null; exit 0

hooks.prompt: Sensitive file guard: BLOCK edits to .env (but allow .env.example), any path under archive/ if your team treats it as immutable, *.lock, poetry.lock, package-lock.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, or secrets directories. ALLOW normal source and docs. Reply BLOCK: <reason> or ALLOW.

If the edit touches src/**/*.py, scan the NEW text only for obvious secret patterns: AWS access keys, private keys (BEGIN PRIVATE KEY), and long base64 blobs labeled as tokens. BLOCK if a new secret is being inserted. ALLOW if only references to env vars or already-redacted examples.
If the bash command would commit or push to protected branch main/master, BLOCK. Patterns: git commit on main/master, git push on main/master, git push origin HEAD:main or :master. Otherwise ALLOW.

Session end reminders (WARN only; always ALLOW stop):
1) Update docs/01_Vault/ProjectTemplate/00_System/Next Session Handoff.md with resume point and blockers.
2) If you changed architecture or governance, update Architecture Invariants or add an ADR under docs/01_Vault/ProjectTemplate/01_Decisions/.
3) If this was an investigation, add a note under docs/01_Vault/ProjectTemplate/06_Investigations/ using 99_Templates/Investigation.md.
